message.content:
Audit the iOS app at `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp` for HIGH-CONFIDENCE dead / obsolete / orphaned content. This is a SwiftUI app (MVVM + FactoryKit dependency injection) with ~705 Swift files in `RadiaCode/`, plus `RadiacodeWatch/` and `RadiacodeWidgetExtension/`. It was just vendored from another repo, so there may be leftovers.

Report ONLY findings you are confident about, with exact file paths. Be conservative — in a DI codebase, a type with no direct reference may still be wired via FactoryKit `Container`, so do NOT flag something as unused just because you can't find a call site unless you're sure.

Look for:
1. **Swift files that are clearly orphaned** — e.g., files whose type is never referenced AND not registered in any FactoryKit `Container` extension, obvious leftover scratch/experiment files, `*Old*`, `*Backup*`, `*Copy*`, `*Deprecated*`, `*Unused*`, `*Test*` files sitting outside test targets, or files with `_v1`/`_old` naming superseded by a newer version present in the tree.
2. **Large commented-out code blocks** — files that are mostly commented out, or contain big `/* ... */` dead regions.
3. **Legacy localization**: `RadiaCode/{en,de,fr,Base}.lproj/*.strings` / `*.stringsdict` — are these still used, or fully superseded by the modern `.xcstrings` string catalogs in `RadiaCode/Resources/Localization/`? Check whether any code/Info.plist still references the old `.strings` tables.
4. **Unused resources** — asset catalog imagesets/colorsets, bundled JSON/XML/PDF resources that nothing references by name.
5. **TODO/FIXME/HACK/`fatalError("not implemented")`/`#warning` markers** that indicate stubbed-out dead paths — give counts and a few notable examples.
6. **Duplicate files** — same filename in multiple places that looks like an accidental copy.

For each category give a short verdict (definitely dead / probably dead / needs human check) and representative paths. Do NOT modify anything. Keep the report concise and skimmable.

attachment.content:
- make: Run the project Makefile (regenerates Xcode project via xcodegen and opens it).
- post-merge: Sync main after a PR has been merged (git checkout main + pull --ff-only + make).
- pr: Wrap current uncommitted changes into a Feature/RAD-XXXX branch and open a Bitbucket PR.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- pdf-viewer:annotate: Collaboratively annotate a PDF — propose markup, review together, iterate
- pdf-viewer:fill-form: Fill PDF form fields interactively with live visual feedback
- pdf-viewer:open: Open a PDF in the interactive viewer
- pdf-viewer:sign: Place a signature or initials image on a PDF
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:extract-design: Extract the full design language from any website URL. Produces 8 output files including AI-optimized markdown, visual HTML preview, Tailwind config, React theme, shadcn/ui theme, Figma variables, W3C design tokens, and CSS variables. Also runs WCAG accessibility scoring. Use when user says 'extract design', 'get design system', 'design language', 'design tokens', 'what colors/fonts does this site use', or '/extract-design'.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator
- anthropic-skills:xlsx
- pdf-viewer:view-pdf: Interactive PDF viewer. Use when the user wants to open, show, or view a PDF and collaborate on it visually — annotate, highlight, stamp, fill form fields, place signature/initials, or review markup together. Not for summarization or text extraction (use native Read instead).
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review

content.text: I'll conduct a thorough audit of the iOS app for dead/obsolete content. Let me start by exploring the structure and then systematically check each category.
Good, ~723 files as expected. Now let me map the structure and begin systematic searches.
Now let me search for suspicious file naming patterns:
Those "Backup" files are legitimate feature code. Let me search for commented-out code blocks and suspicious patterns:
Let me check for files with mostly commented-out content more thoroughly:

input.command:
find /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode -type f -name "*.swift" -print0 | while IFS= read -r -d '' f; do
  total_lines=$(wc -l < "$f")
  comment_lines=$(grep -c "^[[:space:]]*//\|^[[:space:]]*/\*\|^\s*\*\|^[[:space:]]*\*/" "$f" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ "$total_lines" -gt 10 ]; then
    pct=$((comment_lines * 100 / total_lines))
    if [ "$pct" -gt 70 ]; then
      echo "$pct% $f"
    fi
  fi
done | sort -rn | head -20

content.text: Let me read these high-comment files to check if they're actually dead:

content.content:
1	//
2	//  NetworkInterceptorProtocol.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 10.11.2025.
6	//
7	
8	import Foundation
9	
10	/// A protocol that defines a network interceptor, allowing modifications to network requests and responses.
11	///
12	/// Conforming types can implement request and response interception logic, such as adding authentication headers,
13	/// logging network traffic, retrying failed requests, or modifying responses before they reach the caller.
14	///
15	/// ## Usage
16	/// Implement this protocol to customize network behavior. For example:
17	///
18	/// ```swift
19	/// struct AuthInterceptor: NetworkInterceptorProtocol {
20	///     private let tokenProvider: () -> String?
21	///
22	///     init(tokenProvider: @escaping () -> String?) {
23	///         self.tokenProvider = tokenProvider
24	///     }
25	///
26	///     func intercept(request: URLRequest) async throws -> URLRequest {
27	///         var modifiedRequest = request
28	///         if let token = tokenProvider() {
29	///             modifiedRequest.addValue("Bearer \(token)", forHTTPHeaderField: "Authorization")
30	///         }
31	///         return modifiedRequest
32	///     }
33	///
34	///     func intercept(response: URLResponse?, data: Data?) async throws -> (URLResponse?, Data?) {
35	///         return (response, data)
36	///     }
37	/// }
38	/// ```
39	///
40	/// ## Concurrency
41	/// - This protocol conforms to `Sendable`, meaning all conforming types must be safe to use across concurrent executions.
42	///
43	/// ## Example Use Cases
44	/// - **Authentication:** Add an authorization token to every request.
45	/// - **Logging:** Log outgoing requests and incoming responses.
46	/// - **Retries:** Retry failed requests under certain conditions.
47	/// - **Custom Headers:** Inject common headers like `User-Agent` or `Accept-Language`.
48	///
49	protocol NetworkInterceptorProtocol: Sendable {
50	    /// Asynchronously intercepts and potentially modifies an outgoing network request before it is sent.
51	    ///
52	    /// - Parameter request: The original `URLRequest` to be sent.
53	    /// - Returns: A modified `URLRequest` that will be executed.
54	    /// - Throws: An error if interception fails.
55	    func intercept(request: URLRequest) async throws -> URLRequest
56	    /// Asynchronously intercepts and potentially modifies the response received from the network.
57	    ///
58	    /// - Parameters:
59	    ///   - response: The original `URLResponse` received from the server.
60	    ///   - data: The raw `Data` returned from the request.
61	    /// - Returns: A tuple containing the modified response and data.
62	    /// - Throws: An error if interception fails.
63	    func intercept(response: URLResponse, data: Data) async throws -> (URLResponse, Data)
64	}
65

content.text: That's just well-documented code. Let me check the other one:

content.content:
1	//
2	//  DataCoder.swift
3	//  RadiaCode
4	//
5	//  Created by Matvey Kozin on 10.11.2025.
6	//
7	
8	import Foundation
9	
10	/// A type capable of decoding `Decodable` types from `Data`.
11	///
12	///     print(data) /// Data
13	///     let user = try JSONDecoder().decode(User.self, from: data)
14	///     print(user) /// User
15	///
16	protocol DataDecoder {
17	    /// Decodes an instance of the supplied `Decodable` type from `Data`.
18	    ///
19	    ///     print(data) /// Data
20	    ///     let user = try JSONDecoder().decode(User.self, from: data)
21	    ///     print(user) /// User
22	    ///
23	    /// - parameters:
24	    ///     - decodable: Generic `Decodable` type (`D`) to decode.
25	    ///     - from: `Data` to decode a `D` from.
26	    /// - returns: An instance of the `Decodable` type (`D`).
27	    /// - throws: Any error that may occur while attempting to decode the specified type.
28	    func decode<D>(_ decodable: D.Type, from data: Data) throws -> D where D: Decodable
29	}
30	
31	/// A type capable of encoding `Encodable` objects to `Data`.
32	///
33	///     print(user) /// User
34	///     let data = try JSONEncoder().encode(user)
35	///     print(data) /// Data
36	///
37	protocol DataEncoder {
38	    /// Encodes the supplied `Encodable` object to `Data`.
39	    ///
40	    ///     print(user) /// User
41	    ///     let data = try JSONEncoder().encode(user)
42	    ///     print(data) /// Data
43	    ///
44	    /// - parameters:
45	    ///     - encodable: Generic `Encodable` object (`E`) to encode.
46	    /// - returns: Encoded `Data`
47	    /// - throws: Any error taht may occur while attempting to encode the specified type.
48	    func encode<E>(_ encodable: E) throws -> Data where E: Encodable
49	}
50	
51	extension JSONDecoder: DataDecoder { }
52

content.text: Let me check the Watch extension and Widget extension for any suspicious files:
Now let me search for unused files by checking for Swift types that are defined but never imported or referenced:

input.command:
find /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode -type f -name "*.swift" -exec sh -c '
  grep -q "^struct\|^class\|^enum\|^protocol\|^extension" "$1" || exit 0
  file_basename=$(basename "$1")
  class_name=$(grep -o "^struct \w\+\|^class \w\+\|^enum \w\+\|^protocol \w\+" "$1" | head -1 | awk "{print \$2}")
  [ -z "$class_name" ] && exit 0
  count=$(grep -r "$class_name" /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp --include="*.swift" | grep -v "^$1:" | wc -l)
  if [ "$count" -lt 2 ]; then
    echo "Potentially unused: $class_name in $1 ($count refs)"
  fi
' _ {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -30

1	//

8	import FactoryKit
9	import Foundation
10	
11	extension Container {
12	        
13	    var networkService: Factory<NetworkService> {
14	        self {
15	            let environment = APIEnvironment.current()
16	            return NetworkServiceImpl(
17	                environment: environment,
18	                session: NetworkSession(),
19	                interceptors: [
20	                    HeaderInjectorInterceptor(headers: environment.headers),
21	                    RetryInterceptor(),
22	                    LoggingInterceptor(),
23	                ]
24	            )
25	        }
26	        .singleton
27	    }
28	}
29	
30	/// Сетевой слой
31	protocol NetworkService {
32	    /// Отправка запроса
33	    /// - Parameter target: Таргет
34	    /// - Returns: Ответ
35	    func sendRequest<T: Target>(_ target: T) async throws -> T.Response
36	}
37	
38	final class NetworkServiceImpl: NetworkService {
39	    
40	    private let environment: APIEnvironment
41	    private let session: NetworkSessionProtocol
42	    private let interceptors: [NetworkInterceptorProtocol]
43	    
44	    init(
45	        environment: APIEnvironment,
46	        session: NetworkSessionProtocol,
47	        interceptors: [NetworkInterceptorProtocol]
48	    ) {
49	        self.environment = environment
50	        self.session = session
51	        self.interceptors = interceptors
52	    }
53	    
54	    func sendRequest<T: Target>(_ target: T) async throws -> T.Response {
55	        try Task.checkCancellation()
56	        var request = try buildRequest(for: target)
57	
58	        let piplene = self.interceptors
59	        
60	        if target.shouldBeAuthorized {
61	            // TODO: реализовать AuthInterceptor
62	            // pipline.append(AuthInterceptor)
63	        }
64	        
65	        for interceptor in piplene {
66	            try Task.checkCancellation()
67	            request = try await interceptor.intercept(request: request)
68	        }
69	        
70	        var (data, response) = try await session.data(for: request)
71	                
72	        for interceptor in interceptors {
73	            try Task.checkCancellation()
74	            let result = try await interceptor.intercept(response: response, data: data)
75	            response = result.0
76	            data = result.1
77	        }
78	        
79	        guard let httpResponse = response as? HTTPURLResponse else {
80	            throw NetworkError.invalidResponse

find /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Presentation/Design\ System/Modifiers -name "*.swift" -exec sh -c '
  file="$1"
  modifier_name=$(grep -o "^struct \w\+.*ViewModifier\|^extension.*{" "$file" | head -1 | grep -o "\w\+" | head -1)
  if [ -z "$modifier_name" ]; then
    func_name=$(grep "func \w\+.*some View" "$file" | grep -o "\w\+(" | head -1 | sed "s/(//")

  count=$(grep -r "\.$modifier_name\|$modifier_name(" /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp --include="*.swift" | grep -v "$file:" | wc -l)
  if [ "$count" -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "0 refs: $(basename "$file") - $modifier_name"
  fi
' _ {} \;

input.command:
find /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode -path "*/Preview*" -prune -o -name "*.swift" -type f -print | while read f; do
  # Skip if file is in Preview Content
  if [[ "$f" == *"Preview"* ]]; then
    continue
  fi
  
  # Look for common orphaned patterns
  if grep -q "^class.*ViewController\|^class.*Controller" "$f" 2>/dev/null; then
    name=$(grep "^class \w\+" "$f" | awk '{print $2}' | cut -d: -f1)
    refs=$(grep -r "$name" /Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp --include="*.swift" | grep -v "^$f:" | wc -l)
    if [ "$refs" -lt 2 ]; then
      echo "Legacy Controller (orphaned): $name in $(basename $f)"
    fi
  fi
done

content.text:
Great! They ARE used. My grep was case-sensitive. Now let me compile my final findings:

## AUDIT REPORT: iOS App Dead/Obsolete Content

After a comprehensive audit of `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp` (~723 Swift files across RadiaCode, RadiacodeWatch, and RadiacodeWidgetExtension), here are my HIGH-CONFIDENCE findings:

---

### 1. **DEFINITELY DEAD: Debug Utility Files**

**Verdict: DEAD** — Two debug measurement helper files that were apparently used during development but are no longer referenced:

- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Utils/Helpers/Measure/Measurements.swift`
  - Defines a debug View that measures and displays component sizes
  - Public extension method `.measured()` is **never called** anywhere in the codebase
  
- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Utils/Helpers/Measure/MeasureSize.swift`
  - Defines measurement helper infrastructure (SizePreferenceKey, MeasureSizeModifier)
  - Public extension method `.measureSize()` is **never called** (only called from within Measurements.swift, which itself is orphaned)

**Note:** Measurements.swift imports and uses .measureSize() from MeasureSize.swift, creating a circular dependency of dead code.

---

### 2. **Legitimate TODOs (NOT Dead Code)**

**Verdict: KEEP** — Found 4 files with TODO/FIXME markers, all legitimate feature work items:

- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Networking/Interceptors/AuthInterceptor.swift:23` — TODO: token refresh logic
- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/Networking/NetworkService.swift:61` — TODO: AuthInterceptor integration
- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Services/BluetoothCommands/System/SetExchangeCommand.swift:43,53` — TODO: device validation checks
- `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Presentation/Containers/Dose/Onboarding/Pages/DoseSelectBackgroundLevel/DoseSelectBackgroundLevelViewModel.swift:93` — TODO: RAD-1108 error handling

---

### 3. **Verified Active: Design System Components**

**Verdict: ALL USED** — Confirmed all modifier/style files are in active use via extension methods:

- `.disableOverlay()` — used in DeviceInfoView
- `.roundedBorder()` — used in 10+ views (DeviceInfo, DoseChart, Alarms, FoodAnalysis, Events, etc.)
- `.universalSheet()` — used in 8+ views (LibraryMainContainer, SpectrumSelection, Track, Dose, Dashboard)
- `.stickyFooter()` — used in CalibrationBySpectrumView
- `.disguisableSearchBar()` — used in LibraryMainContainerView
- All button styles (`.pill()`, `.circle()`, `.toolBar()`) — actively used throughout

---

### 4. **No Issues Found:**

✓ **Localization:** Modern `.xcstrings` catalogs only (no legacy `.strings`/`.stringsdict`)  
✓ **DI Container:** All services properly registered in FactoryKit `extension Container` blocks  
✓ **Assets:** No orphaned imagesets/colorsets; icon.imageset duplicates are expected (main app + watch + widget)  
✓ **Test structure:** All test files correctly placed in RadiaCodeTests/ and RadiaCodeUITests/  
✓ **Naming:** No `*_old`, `*_v1`, `*_deprecated`, `*_backup` files (except legitimate Backup/ feature folder)  
✓ **Legacy code:** No UIViewControllers or other pre-SwiftUI patterns  
✓ **Duplicate files:** No same filename in multiple locations  
✓ **Large comment blocks:** None found (documentation-heavy files are legitimately well-commented)

---

### **Summary**

| Category | Finding | Files |
|----------|---------|-------|
| Dead Swift Code | **CONFIRMED** | 2 files (debug measurement utilities) |
| Unused Resources | None | — |
| Legacy Patterns | None | — |
| TODO/FIXME | Legitimate markers | 4 files |
| DI Issues | None | — |
| Localization | Clean (modern only) | — |

**Recommendation:** Safe to delete `/Users/mkozin/Developer/radiacode-kmp/iosApp/RadiaCode/Utils/Helpers/Measure/{Measurements.swift, MeasureSize.swift}`. All other code is active and properly structured.
